commit.message:
fix(#144): scrollToItem/animateScrollToItem with scrollOffset=Int.MAX_VALUE (#149)

Scrolls to true bottom of the list, not top of last item.
For messages taller than the viewport, the previous calls landed
mid-message. Int.MAX_VALUE is clamped by LazyColumn layout to the
maximum valid offset — the actual end of scroll range.

Co-authored-by: NickMonrad <nick@lab3.com.au>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: binary mode for luac helper on Windows + correct bytecode header test offset

- Set stdin/stdout to _O_BINARY on Windows in luac_helper.c to prevent
  \n -> \r\n text mode conversion corrupting bytecode output
- Fix test assertions to check byte 15 (sizeof lua_Integer) instead of
  byte 17 (which is LUAC_INT data, not a size field)
- The old tests accidentally passed on Windows due to text mode shifting
  bytes by +2, making byte 17 coincidentally hold sizeof(lua_Integer)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

refactor(llm): replace raw httpx with LiteLLM

Use litellm.acompletion() instead of manual httpx POST to
/v1/chat/completions. This enables 100+ LLM providers out of the box
(OpenAI, Anthropic, Ollama, Gemini, Mistral, Azure, vLLM, etc.).

- llm_client: use litellm.acompletion(), drop httpx import
- config: replace LLM_BASE_URL/LLM_API_KEY with LLM_MODEL (litellm
  format e.g. 'ollama/mistral', 'gpt-4o') and optional LLM_API_BASE
- docker-compose: update env vars for new config
- README: update config table with LiteLLM model format and provider docs

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: install.sh reads VERSION dynamically after download

Hardcoded VERSION serves as fallback for --help banner.
After downloading the repo, reads VERSION file to display actual version.
Eliminates need to manually update install.sh on each release.

commit.message:
refactor: extract descent loop into named phase functions

Main loop is now 10 lines calling named phases:
  runImplementorPhase() → runEvaluatorPhase() → runEscalation() → runTerminatorPhase()

Extracted 6 functions from the 465-line monolith:
- resumeOrInitState() — resume/init logic
- runImplementorPhase() — research + plan + exec with error checks
- runEvaluatorPhase() — evaluate + git commit/revert + record
- runEscalation() — campaigns + radical plan
- runTerminatorPhase() — rule check + agentic terminator
- handleIterationError() — revert + error report

All share a LoopContext struct. No behavior changes.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

list, and skill disable. Server handles list_capabilities,
toggle_mcp, and toggle_skill WebSocket messages.

feat: embed Lua 5.3.6 compiler — no external luac dependency

- Bundle Lua 5.3.6 C source and build 32-bit/64-bit helper executables
  at compile time via cc crate (same approach as luatos-tools)
- Helpers are embedded via include_bytes!() and cached at runtime in
  user's local app data / XDG cache directory
- Replace find_luac() external search with embedded compiler invocation
- compile_lua_bytes() API: source bytes in, bytecode out via stdin/stdout
- Flash commands (bk7258, xt804) now read bitw/use-luac/use-debug from
  SOC info.json and compile .lua files automatically during flash
- Add SocInfo convenience methods: script_bitw(), script_use_luac(),
  script_strip_debug() with chip-based defaults (XT804→64, BK72xx→32)
- Add tests for 32/64-bit compilation, strip mode, syntax errors
- Verified on real Air8101 hardware (COM6): embedded luac compiles and
  flashes correctly

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix %ThrowTypeError% intrinsic: dispatch, frozen, non-extensible

- Add 'Function.prototype.restrictedThrow' handler to call_host_fn
  so the thrower actually throws TypeError when called directly
- Freeze the %ThrowTypeError% object: non-writable/non-configurable
  name and length, __frozen__, __sealed__, __non_extensible__ flags
- Same thrower instance shared for getter and setter (per spec)

Cluster 38 (ThrowTypeError): 7 → 1 failure (6 tests fixed)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: add mihomo-proxy-setup skill

Add a new skill for installing and managing a Mihomo-based proxy in
user-space. Supports Linux (systemd) and macOS (launchd), with:

- Mihomo binary + yq auto-download for correct OS/arch
- Clash subscription integration with auto-update timer
- MetaCubeXD Web UI panel
- Proxy wrapper scripts (with-proxy, proxy-agent, proxy-copilot, proxy-claude)
- Interactive parameter collection (ports, secret, subscription URL)
- Full uninstall flow
- Troubleshooting guide

No root/sudo required.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: ToggleSelected deletes schedule, RemoveAsync leaks config, dead DI

Bug 1 (severe): ScheduleForm.ToggleSelected called RemoveScheduleAsync to
disable a schedule. RemoveScheduleAsync previously called
_configService.DeleteSchedule — so toggling a schedule off permanently erased
it from configuration. Fix: ToggleSelected now always calls
AddOrUpdateScheduleAsync (which handles both enable and disable paths in
Quartz without touching config). Falls back to SaveSchedule if no scheduler.

Bug 2 (concern separation): SyncSchedulerService.RemoveScheduleAsync was
calling _configService.DeleteSchedule. Callers that want to delete from
config already do so explicitly (ScheduleForm.DeleteSelected). Removing the
call from RemoveScheduleAsync keeps each layer responsible for its own
persistence. DeleteSelected still calls _configService.DeleteSchedule
explicitly before removing the Quartz job.

Bug 3 (dead code): Program.cs registered ScheduleForm as a transient DI
service, but ScheduleForm is always constructed with 'new' in ShowSchedulesDialog
with explicit deps. The DI registration was never resolved and is removed.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: SyncAsync double-processes conflicts via ExecuteSyncAsync

SyncAsync() resolved conflicts and moved them to plan.Uploads/Downloads,
then called ExecuteSyncAsync(). ExecuteSyncAsync calls ResolveConflictsAsync,
which checks plan.HasConflicts (= plan.Conflicts.Count > 0). Because
plan.Conflicts was never cleared, ResolveConflictsAsync fired
ConflictsDetected again and appended duplicate Upload/Download operations.

This affected the SyncJob (scheduled sync) code path.
The MainForm two-step path (CalculateChangesAsync + ExecuteSyncAsync) was
unaffected because it never called SyncAsync.

Fix: clear plan.Conflicts after SyncAsync finishes processing them and
before calling ExecuteSyncAsync.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: ConflictResolutionForm ApplyAll does not visually update grid

ApplyAll() modified row.Resolution on the backing List<ConflictRow> then
called _grid.Refresh(). Because List<T> does not implement
INotifyPropertyChanged, the DataGridViewComboBoxColumn cells retained their
old displayed values — the quick-apply buttons appeared broken.

Fix: reset DataSource to null then reassign the list, forcing the
DataGridView to re-read every cell value from the data source.
The underlying data was always correct; only the visual feedback was wrong.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
security: add Strategy 4 to detect cross-directory attacks

When cwd is outside the project, bash commands with absolute paths
to project files were undetected. Strategy 4 extracts absolute paths
from bash commands and walks up from each to find .agents/ directory.

Now catches: rm /path/to/project/file from home directory.

51/51 tests passed (5 + 15 + 31 including 4 cross-directory tests).

Dark mode:

commit.message:
harness: introduce Feature/Backend/Harness composition layer (Phase 5)

Add the harness package providing a composable orchestration layer
between Kernel and applications:

- Feature interface for pluggable capabilities (tools, hooks, prompts)
- Backend interface unifying Workspace + Executor into a single port
- LocalBackend combining existing workspace and executor types
- Harness struct orchestrating Kernel + Backend + ordered Feature install
- 7 concrete Feature constructors: BootstrapContext, SessionPersistence,
  Checkpointing, TaskDelegation, LLMResilience, ExecutionPolicy,
  PatchToolCalls
- Kernel.Apply() method for post-construction option application
- 16 tests covering install ordering, error propagation, nil handling,
  and all feature constructors

commit.message:
feat(proxy): add OpenAI-compatible GraphWalks proxy

Implements a FastAPI server that intercepts GraphWalk benchmark prompts,
loads graph data into FalkorDB, generates Cypher via a small LLM, executes
queries, and returns results in OpenAI chat completion format.

Components:
- main.py: FastAPI app with /v1/chat/completions endpoint
- prompt_parser.py: regex-based edge/question extractor
- graph_manager.py: FalkorDB lifecycle (create, bulk load, query, delete)
- llm_client.py: Text-to-Cypher LLM orchestrator with safety validation
- executor.py: end-to-end pipeline tying all components together
- models.py: Pydantic models for OpenAI API compatibility
- config.py: environment-based configuration
- Dockerfile + docker-compose.yml: containerized deployment
- 14 unit tests for prompt parser

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add full 2024 AFL squad rosters with 35-38 players per team

Create afl_rosters_2024.py with complete squad rosters for all 18 AFL
teams from the 2024 season. Each team includes the starting 22 (matching
afl_teams_2024.py exactly) plus 13-16 extended squad/depth players.

Features:
- 18 teams with 35-38 players each (649 total players)
- Starting 22 skill ratings match existing afl_teams_2024.py exactly
- Durability ratings (1-99) based on games played availability
- Extended squad players have natural position assignments
- Backup ruckmen with appropriate ruck_craft ratings
- create_all_rosters() and get_roster() utility functions
- No duplicate jersey numbers or player names within teams
